overview: This is a margay (Leopardus wiedii), a small wild cat native to the forests of Central and South America. They are highly adapted for an arboreal life, possessing specialized ankles that can rotate 180 degrees, allowing them to climb down trees headfirst. Margays are listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss and historic hunting for the wildlife trade. They are nocturnal and solitary hunters, primarily preying on small mammals, birds, and reptiles. Wikipedia +3 4 sites Margay - Wikipedia

This is a margay (Leopardus wiedii), a small wild cat native to the forests of Central and South America.
They are highly adapted for an arboreal life, possessing specialized ankles that can rotate 180 degrees, allowing them to climb down trees headfirst.
Margays are listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss and historic hunting for the wildlife trade.
They are nocturnal and solitary hunters, primarily preying on small mammals, birds, and reptiles.
